semanticCore: Proximity, source, presence, soft, or from
quranicRelevance: Special adverbial noun indicating close source/presence ('ladun' - 'min ladunnee', 'min ladunka').

keyDerivations.meaning: With, near, from the presence of, or from the side of

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَعَلَّمْنَاهُ مِنْ لَدُنَّا عِلْمًا (18:65): And We had taught him from Our presence/source a knowledge (special divine source).
summaryEn: The root serves as an adverbial particle of close proximity, describing gifts, mercy, and knowledge sourced directly from the divine presence.
summaryAr: يخدم هذا الجذر كظرف لدلالة القرب اللدني الشديد، واصفاً المعارف والرحمات اللدنية الممنوحة مباشرة من الخالق للعبد.
rawText: لَدْنٌ Supple; lithe; limber; limp; pliant; pliable; flexible. مِنْ لَدُنْ From the time of: see a verse cited in art. عى, conj. 4. === هٰذَا الأَمْرُ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ i. q. مِنْ قِبَلِهِ, q. v. (Lth in TA, in art. قبل.)
